items.title: Germany cracks down on employees calling in sick by phone
items.summary: Chucking a sickie is no longer as easy as a quick phone call in Germany, under new laws hoped to boost productivity. Here's how the world is responding.

items.title: WorkSafe investigating after Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald suffers life-ending head injuries
items.summary: Victoria's workplace safety watchdog is investigating after a suburban footy player was injured, and later died, after his head collided with a cricket pitch.

items.title: Activists urge Australia to sign treaty to ban nuclear weapons
items.summary: There are renewed calls for Australia to sign a treaty ensuring nuclear weapons can't ever be facilitated, stored or manufactured in Australia.

items.title: One in 25 teens affected by AI-assisted online sexual abuse, study finds
items.summary: About one in every 25 Australians under the age of 18 has been the victim or knows a victim of artificial intelligence-assisted online sexual abuse, according to a new study.

items.title: Donald Trump teaches crypto devotees a valuable lesson
items.summary: US President Donald Trump should have been a godsend to the mysterious world of cryptocurrency. Instead, it has been a godsend to him.

items.title: Use your phone for work? This is how you can claim it as a tax deduction
items.summary: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

items.title: Too cold to exercise? Here's how to start and stay on track
items.summary: Experts say winter is a good time to double down on exercise rather than taking the foot off the gas.

items.title: No subscriptions? No problems! Here are the best movies and TV series you can watch free
items.summary: From Bluey in Yolŋu Matha to the first feature film directed by an Indigenous woman, there is a trove of free movies and TV to dive into this NAIDOC Week.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
items.summary: Your challenge in today's ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

items.title: US crashes out as Belgium gets the last laugh after controversy
items.summary: Belgium completes a thumping 4-1 win over the United States, who started Folarin Balogun after his controversial reprieve from FIFA.

items.title: Trump confirms he asked FIFA to review US player's red card, as UEFA says U-turn 'crossed a red line'
items.summary: Football's European governing body blasts the decision to suspend a US striker's World Cup red-card ban, saying FIFA had "crossed a red line" after US President Donald Trump personally intervened in the case.

items.title: China warns Australia over military alliance with Fiji that promises to 'act to meet the common danger'
items.summary: China warns Australia not to harm its interests in the Pacific after Anthony Albanese strikes a landmark military alliance with Fiji.

items.title: Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald dies after suffering head injury during game
items.summary: Footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald has died after suffering critical head injuries during a reserves game in the city's north on the weekend.

items.title: Social media giants front royal commission into antisemitism
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Fires rage in Kyiv after Russian missile, drone attack
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Central Australian tourism funding injection celebrated after several challenging years
items.summary: After several challenging years for tourism operators in central Australia, there is hope an incentive of free flights for visitors and a new grant fund will supercharge tourism in the region.

items.title: Hobart Deputy Lord Mayor Zelinda Sherlock under fire over taking office snacks
items.summary: Despite her apology and the integrity commission dismissing the case, Hobart's deputy mayor is still facing backlash after she took snacks from council premises for her home office.

items.title: AFP says young person formally warned over online threats made to Indian Prime Minister Modi
items.summary: Australian Federal Police says a young person was identified and issued a formal warning over an online threat made towards Mr Modi, ahead of his official visit later this week.

items.title: Edge Early Learning Gawler East closed for second time after child taken to hospital
items.summary: South Australia's Education Standards Board says it has "serious concerns" about an Edge Early Learning centre after a child was taken to hospital following an allergen incident, with the centre directed to close immediately for the second time this year.

items.title: New all-time Australian air pressure record under intense winter weather system
items.summary: Preliminary data shows the highest ever pressure in Australia was recorded in Tasmania on Monday morning.

items.title: 'Pink army' gathers in Rundle Mall to welcome home victorious Thunderbirds
items.summary: Hundreds of fans have flocked to Rundle Mall, dressed in pink, to welcome back the Adelaide Thunderbirds following an unforgettable grand final win.

items.title: Court told 'Razor' scooter used to murder axe-wielding man near Newcastle
items.summary: A Supreme Court jury hears a Razor-branded scooter was the alleged murder weapon used to kill a man who was armed with an axe during a violent brawl north of Newcastle.

items.title: Police investigating suspicious death of 17-year-old girl in remote NT community of Galiwin'ku
items.summary: Northern Territory Police say the girl was found inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, and was declared dead at the scene. A 34-year-old man known to the woman has been arrested.

items.title: 'Assassination plot' impeachment trial involving Duterte dynasty begins in Manila
items.summary: A highly consequential impeachment trial against Philippines Vice-President Sara Duterte could spell the end for her political career and shape the nation's 2028 election.

items.title: Comedian's 'terrifying' roadside assistance ordeal sparks national change
items.summary: A woman's campaign for change after her own frightening experience being broken down outside Canberra will see NRMA roadside assistance contractors required to provide a code to customers to verify their identity.

items.title: England's World Cup Azteca thriller a stunning deliverance from ghosts of the past
items.summary: England teams of old would have buckled under the pressure put upon them by Mexico and their 80,000-strong band of supporters at the Azteca. But this England team turned a nightmare into a dreamland, at a venue haunted by so many ghosts of the past.

items.title: Mabil says monoculture debate 'bulls***' as Socceroos return home from FIFA World Cup
items.summary: The Socceroos star says anyone "trying to divide" the community is "probably not Australian themselves".

items.title: Four of our best self-saucing puddings for winter
items.summary: You'd never guess it but self-saucing puddings are so easy to make. Here are four of our best for you try while the weather is cool.

items.title: Socceroo Awer Mabil speaks on his team's multiculturalism
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: England star hurt in celebrations, Erling Haaland surprises himself
items.summary: England revels in a famous victory over Mexico, but the celebrations come at a cost for one star, while Erling Haaland's brilliance even has the man himself dumbfounded. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

items.title: Sophie Molineux praised after leading Australia to Women's T20 World Cup win
items.summary: Sophie Molineux is praised for her leadership ability after guiding Australia to its Women's T20 World Cup victory.

items.title: Rare humpback whale calf, believed to be a leucistic, spotted off NSW and Queensland coast
items.summary: The calf, thought to be one of only a dozen in the Southern Hemisphere, has been spotted swimming alongside its mother.

items.title: SANFL avoids strike action after months of negotiations over pay
items.summary: SANFL Players' Association members have voted for a memorandum of understanding with the state league amid an ongoing decline in players' pay.

items.title: Police search underway after 36yo man fatally stabbed at Taree
items.summary: Police are asking for public help to find the person who fatally stabbed a man found at a home at Taree on the NSW Mid North Coast early this morning.

items.title: World Cup: Ice-cold Erling Haaland with the moments that matter to send Brazil crashing
items.summary: Brazil has long been trying to re-capture its glory days, but they are fading further into obscurity, while Erling Haaland proves Norway is a genuine World Cup threat in 2-1 win.

items.title: Maroons skipper Cameron Munster focused on State of Origin decider against Blues
items.summary: Cameron Munster wants to honour Queensland State of Origin greats by leading the Maroons to a series victory over NSW in Brisbane on Wednesday night.

items.title: Wildcards from Whyalla return to win H2 Grand Prix after disqualification heartbreak
items.summary: A year after a devastating technical disqualification snatched victory from five South Australian teens at a hydrogen-powered engineering race, they returned as wildcard entrants to the 2026 H2 Grand Prix Finals in Switzerland.

items.title: Australia's Space agency says beach objects likely fuel pressure vessels from launch vehicle
items.summary: Australia's Space Agency says objects found on a north Queensland beach are likely pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle.

items.title: Nigel Farage referred to UK standards watchdog over undeclared gifts from crypto investor George Cottrell
items.summary: Nigel Farage was reportedly provided with security services, social media support and accommodation by George Cottrell, a 32-year-old crypto investor who previously served eight months in prison for wire fraud.

items.title: Alleged Mongols bikie gang associate on the run after escaping custody during Darwin medical transfer
items.summary: Police are searching for remand prisoner Isaac Thompson, 26, who they say is a "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw motorcycle gang, after he escaped custody during a medical transfer on Sunday night.

items.title: Humpback whale freed from fishing gear on NSW Central Coast
items.summary: Whale rescuers say there have been 24 entanglements already this migration season. Meanwhile, a small whale was euthanised after it stranded itself on a beach near Newcastle, despite rescue efforts from residents and experts.

items.title: Meta policy director downplays claims policy change led to more antisemitic content
items.summary: A Meta executive has downplayed claims a change in the company's policies led to a rise in antisemitism while giving evidence to the royal commission.

items.title: New cancer care program empowers Yarrabah residents undergoing treatment
items.summary: A new program in Far North Queensland aims to make life easier for people receiving treatment for cancer, while reducing the number of people who develop the disease.

items.title: WA minister Paul Papalia quits politics, triggering Secret Harbour by-election
items.summary: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

items.title: Developer quietly sells illegally cleared south Sydney site earmarked for now-rejected mansion
items.summary: In 2020, Amir Abu Abara illegally cleared land in Barden Ridge to build a lavish mansion. After a fine, a replanting works order, and the rejection of his housing plan, he is no longer the site's owner.

items.title: Nathan Fitzgerald's football club calls for investigation into cricket pitches on sports ovals
items.summary: Nathan Fitzgerald's club has asked authorities to look at the way hard cricket pitches are covered on multi-use ovals as the 27-year-old continues to receive end-of-life care.

items.title: Lost teenagers rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park
items.summary: Three teenagers were rescued by police after becoming lost in a national park in Adelaide's south.

items.title: Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic beats Roger Federer record despite 'meltdowns'
items.summary: Novak Djokovic fights off a plucky opponent and his own demons to etch his name into another corner of the record books at Wimbledon.

items.title: Police to investigate whether cannibalism was involved in 4yo's death on Central Coast
items.summary: Cannibalism will form part of an investigation into the death of a four-year-old boy on the NSW Central Coast, with an independent review to take place by the Department of Communities and Justice.

items.title: Great Southern records highest rate of whooping cough cases in regional WA
items.summary: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already more than doubled since last year, with doctors expecting the figure to grow further.

items.title: First Nations creators tell Indigenous stories through video games
items.summary: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

items.title: Safety fears spark calls for better protections for Neil the seal
items.summary: The elephant seal has been attracting onlookers in southern Tasmania, as well as a growing social media audience, but fears for his wellbeing and concerns that some people aren't keeping their distance have led to calls to better protect Neil.

items.title: Anthony Albanese 'unequivocally' apologises for saying he would 'shag' Kylie Minogue during podcast game
items.summary: Anthony Albanese made the comments during a game of "shag, marry, date" on the Bush Deep podcast on Friday, which have been widely condemned as inappropriate.

items.title: Mount Isa barber leaves behind fresh fades and more on Mornington Island
items.summary: Mount Isa barber Jon James travels to remote Mornington Island to teach barbering skills, hoping to leave behind a trade that will last long after he returns home.

items.title: Man charged with unlawful striking after West End park death
items.summary: The 27-year-old Yeronga man allegedly struck a 66-year-old man, causing him to fall and seriously injure his head.

items.title: Wimbledon 2026: Naomi Osaka stuns Aryna Sabalenka to advance to quarterfinals
items.summary: Naomi Osaka downs top seed Aryna Sabalenka in straight sets in a fourth round meeting of grand slam champions at Wimbledon.

items.title: Heritage sheep breeder, 11, helping Hampshire Down variety avoid extinction
items.summary: Mitch Dickens's passion for sheep genetics saw his parents give him his own stud, which they hope will help keep a heritage breed alive.

items.title: China to test ballistic missile in Pacific — as it happened
items.summary: The Chinese government informed governments in the region about the test earlier today, the ABC has been told.

items.title: Mental health service supports new NT parents struggling with depression and anxiety
items.summary: Natalya Wallace thought she just had the baby blues as a result of a traumatic birth experience. When she realised she needed mental health support, the young mother was relieved to find a new service in her rural area.

items.title: World Cup: US striker Folarin Balogun freed to play Belgium despite red card after Donald Trump call
items.summary: US striker Folarin Balogun is free to play Belgium despite earning a red card in his last match, in a shock decision that delights Donald Trump and "astonishes" the Belgians.

items.title: SA Police report to coroner into Somerton Man case complete 'very soon'
items.summary: The man who worked out the identity of the Somerton Man through a DNA match says SA Police has told him it is close to finishing its report for the coroner to finally officially put the 78-year mystery to rest.

items.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese signs Ocean of Peace Alliance with Fiji
items.summary: Australia and Fiji strike a new defence alliance in a major development that could reshape the Pacific's strategic landscape.

items.title: Domestic and family violence victim-survivors at risk of CTE, experts warn
items.summary: Enduring brutal physical abuse can increase the risk of developing a sinister brain disease, yet many victim-survivors are unaware, experts warn.

items.title: World Cup: How man behind Norway's viral Viking row turned team motivation into global sensation
items.summary: Ole Frøystad has always been a passionate football fan and when Norway qualified for its first men's World Cup since 1998, he was inspired to create the iconic chant that's become a global sensation.

items.title: Ten Richmond businesses affected by fire after tobacco shop ramming in inner Melbourne
items.summary: More than 80 firefighters were called to Richmond where multiple businesses were alight after a shop was rammed this morning.

items.title: ABC trials AI writing tools for news staff amid trust warnings
items.summary: Experts and the journalists’ union have welcomed the ABC’s plans to use artificial intelligence responsibly, but warned misuse could damage trust in the public broadcaster.

items.title: AFP to push Australia's Pacific police training model to reduce UN costs
items.summary: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

items.title: Gutsy England prevails over Mexico in instant World Cup classic
items.summary: Ten-man England holds on to defeat Mexico 3-2 in a World Cup classic at the Azteca, with Jude Bellingham's brace and Harry Kane's penalty enough to send the Three Lions through.

items.title: The economy is strong yet consumer sentiment is rock bottom
items.summary: The paradox of a strong economy and stock market with very low consumer sentiment is not close to being resolved. Quite the opposite, the disconnect is, if anything, widening.
